body:
Closes #686.

Responds to the deeper implementation request in [the maintainer review comment](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/689#issuecomment-4973111296).

## Root cause

The first draft implemented a standalone `AssociativeMemory`, but it did not make the existing durable memory, synchronization, dreaming/automatic-learning, browser mirror, or Agent CLI use that policy. Durable events had read counts but no write history, stable-id rewrites could retain stale text, and retention considered only a subset of the issue's incoming/outgoing links-network signals. That made the implementation demonstrative rather than the generalized runtime behavior requested by the issue.

## What changed

### Durable associative auto-learning

- Durable `MemoryEvent` records now carry monotone `accessCount` and `writeCount` values across native storage, import/export, sync merge, substitutions, the browser memory mirror, and link-store projection.
- New knowledge starts with one write; changed knowledge increments writes exactly once; legacy events are backfilled safely.
- Stable-id rewrites persist the new expression text instead of keeping the stale value.
- Automatic dreaming now reconstructs `AssociativeMemory` from real durable events and ranks retention with all four signals:
  `read × reads + write × writes + incoming × in_degree + outgoing × out_degree`.
- Evidence associations are traversed in both directions, so frequently referenced and frequently referencing expressions both influence retention.

### Generalized associative ingestion and recall

- Candidate ingestion preserves qualifiers, canonicalizes legacy/namespaced aliases, deduplicates expressions, and retains validation warnings rather than silently dropping misaligned candidates.
- The links network supports deterministic bounded bidirectional multi-hop recall and charges reads to recalled expressions.
- Links Notation output includes usage counters, qualifiers, validation warnings, associations, degree, and retention scores.
- The implementation remains links-native: links/network terminology and incident links are used throughout rather than graph/edge/vertex abstractions.

### Formal AI through Agent CLI

The task is now routed through the production agentic planner. A generalized document recipe:

1. parses the persisted Links Notation artifact,
2. derives a report from the production durable-memory adapter,
3. writes the result,
4. reads it back for byte verification,
5. returns the verified artifact.

The committed replay is deterministic and tested byte-for-byte:
[Agent CLI session](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/blob/issue-686-d704881f337a/docs/case-studies/issue-686/agent-cli-session-associative-learning.json).

A real external `@link-assistant/agent` run against the release Formal AI server also completed on its first attempt in three chat rounds and produced the committed 1,479-byte report:
[external Agent CLI artifact](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/blob/issue-686-d704881f337a/docs/case-studies/issue-686/agent-cli-external-e2e.lino).

### Research and traceability

The case study now traces R686-01 through R686-18 and repository requirements R445 through R458. It distinguishes what the Wikontic paper actually prescribes—qualified candidate extraction, ontology alignment, alias normalization/deduplication, incremental storage, validation retention, and iterative retrieval—from the issue-specific degree-weighted retention extension.

See the [deep case study](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/tree/issue-686-d704881f337a/docs/case-studies/issue-686), [persistence mapping](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/blob/issue-686-d704881f337a/docs/case-studies/issue-686/persistence-mapping.md), and [solution plans](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/blob/issue-686-d704881f337a/docs/case-studies/issue-686/solution-plans.md).

## Reproduction and regression coverage

The regression tests reproduce the concrete failures from the first draft:

- rewriting an identified expression previously retained stale text;
- durable mutations previously lost write history after serialization/sync;
- dreaming previously ranked only reads and incoming references;
- evidence direction, qualifiers, aliases, warnings, and multi-hop recall were not represented;
- the issue task previously had no production Agent CLI route.

Coverage now includes 21 focused issue-686 tests for durable round trips, sync merge, browser-compatible fields, link projection, both evidence directions, deterministic eviction/recall, qualifiers/warnings, generalized task recognition, derived-not-canned output, tool-loop execution, and exact committed-session replay.

## Verification

- `cargo test --all-features`: integration 88/88; supporting tests 475/475; unit/specification 1,589 passed, 0 failed, 2 ignored; doc tests passed.
- `RUSTFLAGS=-Dwarnings cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`: passed.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`: passed.
- `node --check src/web/memory.js`: passed.
- Web TDZ and hardcoded-UI checks: passed.
- Release binary build: passed.
- Real external Agent CLI E2E: passed on the first attempt, 3 rounds, exact required content.
- `origin/main` is merged and the working tree is clean.
title: Integrate associative auto-learning into durable memory and Agent CLI (#686)
